description.original: Integrate Git version control with automatic backup and other advanced features.
description.translation: Integrate Git version control with automatic backup and other advanced features.

dict.text:\"Whitespace and newlines are inter: text:"Whitespace and newlines are interpreted as part of the document and in changes by default (hence not ignored). This makes the last line being shown as 'changed' when a new subsequent line is added, even if the previously last line's text is the same."
dict.text:\"If you don't care about purely-wh: text:"If you don't care about purely-whitespace changes (e.g. list nesting / quote indentation changes), then activating this will provide more meaningful change detection."
dict.text:\"Supports 'rgb(r,g,b)', 'hsl(h,s,l: text:"Supports 'rgb(r,g,b)', 'hsl(h,s,l)', hex (#) and named colors (e.g. 'black', 'purple'). Color preview: "

dict.setText(`Debugging and logging:\nYou can:
setText(`Debugging and logging:
You can always see the logs of this and every other plugin by opening the console with`)
dict.setText(`Are you sure you want to DELETE: setText(`Are you sure you want to DELETE the ${gu(this.deleteCount,"untracked file")}? They are deleted according to your Obsidian trash settting.`)
dict.setText(`Are you sure you want to discar: setText(`Are you sure you want to discard ALL changes in ${gu(this.discardCount,"tracked file")}?`)
dict.setName(\"Split timers for automatic com: setName("Split timers for automatic commit and sync")
dict.setName(`Specify custom commit message o: setName(`Specify custom commit message on auto ${n}`)
dict.setName(\"List filenames affected by com: setName("List filenames affected by commit in the commit body")
dict.setName(\"Status bar with summary of lin: setName("Status bar with summary of line changes")
dict.setName(\"Automatically refresh source c: setName("Automatically refresh source control view on file changes")
dict.setName(\"Show the count of modified fil: setName("Show the count of modified files in the status bar")
dict.setName(\"Username on your git server. E: setName("Username on your git server. E.g. your username on GitHub")
dict.setName(\"Show commit authoring informat: setName("Show commit authoring information next to each line")
dict.setName(\"Follow movement and copies acr: setName("Follow movement and copies across files and commits")
dict.setName(\"Ignore whitespace and newlines: setName("Ignore whitespace and newlines in changes")
dict.setDesc(\"Enable to use one interval for: setDesc("Enable to use one interval for commit and another for sync.")
dict.setDesc(`${i.settings.differentIntervalC: setDesc(`${i.settings.differentIntervalCommitAndPush?"Commit":"Commit and sync"} changes every X minutes. Set to 0 (default) to disable. (See below setting for further configuration!)`)
dict.setDesc(`If turned on, sets last auto ${: setDesc(`If turned on, sets last auto ${n} timestamp to the latest commit timestamp. This reduces the frequency of auto ${n} when doing manual commits.`)
setDesc("Push commits every X minutes. Set to 0 (default) to disable.")
setDesc("Pull changes every X minutes. Set to 0 (default) to disable.")
dict.setDesc(`If turned on, only staged files: setDesc(`If turned on, only staged files are committed on ${n}. If turned off, all changed files are committed.`)
dict.setDesc(\"You will get a pop up to speci: setDesc("You will get a pop up to specify your message.")
dict.setDesc(\"Available placeholders: {{date: setDesc("Available placeholders: {{date}} (see below), {{hostname}} (see below), {{numFiles}} (number of changed files in the commit) and {{files}} (changed files in commit message).")

setDesc("Available placeholders: {{date}} (see below), {{hostname}} (see below), {{numFiles}} (number of changed files in the commit) and {{files}} (changed files in commit message). Leave empty to require manual input on each commit.")
dict.setDesc(\"A script that is run using 'sh: setDesc("A script that is run using 'sh -c' to generate the commit message. May be used to generate commit messages using AI tools. Available placeholders: {{hostname}}, {{date}}.")
dict.setDesc(\"Specify custom hostname for ev: setDesc("Specify custom hostname for every device. Defaults to the OS hostname if not set on desktop.")
dict.setDesc(\"Decide how to integrate commit: setDesc("Decide how to integrate commits from your remote branch into your local branch.")
dict.setDesc(\"Decide how to solve conflicts: setDesc("Decide how to solve conflicts when pulling remote changes. This can be used to favor your local changes or the remote changes automatically.")
dict.setDesc(\"Automatically pull commits whe: setDesc("Automatically pull commits when Obsidian starts.")
dict.setDesc(\"Commit-and-sync with default s: setDesc("Commit-and-sync with default settings means staging everything -> committing -> pulling -> pushing. Ideally this is a single action that you do regularly to keep your local and remote repository in sync.")
dict.setDesc(`Most of the time you want to pu: setDesc(`Most of the time you want to push after committing. Turning this off turns a commit-and-sync action into commit ${i.settings.pullBeforePush?"and pull ":""}only. It will still be called commit-and-sync.`)
dict.setDesc(`On commit-and-sync, pull commit: setDesc(`On commit-and-sync, pull commits as well. Turning this off turns a commit-and-sync action into commit ${i.settings.disablePush?"":"and push "}only.`)
dict.setDesc(\"Hunks are sections of grouped: setDesc("Hunks are sections of grouped line changes right in your editor.")
dict.setDesc(\"This allows you to see your ch: setDesc("This allows you to see your changes right in your editor via colored markers and stage/reset/preview individual hunks.")
dict.setDesc(\"Adds commands to stage/reset i: setDesc("Adds commands to stage/reset individual Git diff hunks and navigate between them via 'Go to next/prev hunk' commands.")
dict.setDesc(\"Show the author of the commit: setDesc("Show the author of the commit in the history view.")
dict.setDesc(\"Show the date of the commit in: setDesc("Show the date of the commit in the history view. The {{date}} placeholder format is used to display the date.")
dict.setDesc(\"On slower machines this may ca: setDesc("On slower machines this may cause lags. If so, just disable this option.")
dict.setDesc(\"Milliseconds to wait after fil: setDesc("Milliseconds to wait after file change before refreshing the Source Control View.")
dict.setDesc('Set the style for the diff view: setDesc('Set the style for the diff view. Note that the actual diff in "Split" mode is not generated by Git, but the editor itself instead so it may differ from the diff generated by Git. One advantage of this is that you can edit the text in that view.')
dict.setDesc(\"Disable informative notificati: setDesc("Disable informative notifications for git operations to minimize distraction (refer to status bar for updates).")
dict.setDesc(\"Disable error notifications of: setDesc("Disable error notifications of any kind to minimize distraction (refer to status bar for updates).")
dict.setDesc(\"Don't show notifications when: setDesc("Don't show notifications when there are no changes to commit or push.")
dict.setDesc(\"Obsidian must be restarted for: setDesc("Obsidian must be restarted for the changes to take affect.")
dict.setDesc('Add \"Stage\", \"Unstage\" and: setDesc('Add "Stage", "Unstage" and "Add to .gitignore" actions to the file menu.')
dict.setDesc(\"Type in your password. You won: setDesc("Type in your password. You won't be able to see it again.")
dict.setDesc(\"These settings usually don't n: setDesc("These settings usually don't need to be changed, but may be required for special setups.")
dict.setDesc('\"Commit-and-sync\" and \"pull\: setDesc('"Commit-and-sync" and "pull" takes care of submodules. Missing features: Conflicted files, count of pulled/pushed/committed files. Tracking branch needs to be set for each submodule.')
dict.setDesc(\"Whenever a checkout happens on: setDesc("Whenever a checkout happens on the root repository, recurse the checkout on the submodules (if the branches exist).")
dict.setDesc(\"Specify the path to the Git bi: setDesc("Specify the path to the Git binary/executable. Git should already be in your PATH. Should only be necessary for a custom Git installation.")
dict.setDesc(\"Use each line for a new enviro: setDesc("Use each line for a new environment variable in the format KEY=VALUE .")
dict.setDesc(\"Use each line for one path\"): setDesc("Use each line for one path")
dict.setDesc(\"Removing previously added envi: setDesc("Removing previously added environment variables will not take effect until Obsidian is restarted.")
dict.setDesc(`\n Sets the relative:
setDesc(`
            Sets the relative path to the vault from which the Git binary should be executed.
             Mostly used to set the path to the Git repository, which is only required if the Git repository is below the vault root directory. Use "\\" instead of "/" on Windows.
            `)
dict.setDesc('Corresponds to the GIT_DIR envi: setDesc('Corresponds to the GIT_DIR environment variable. Requires restart of Obsidian to take effect. Use "\\" instead of "/" on Windows.')
dict.setDesc(\"Disables the plugin on this de: setDesc("Disables the plugin on this device. This setting is not synced.")
dict.setDesc(\"If you like this Plugin, consi: setDesc("If you like this Plugin, consider donating to support continued development.")
dict.setDesc(\"If and how the author is displ: setDesc("If and how the author is displayed")
dict.setDesc(\"If and how the date and time o: setDesc("If and how the date and time of authoring the line is displayed")
